scope.claim_limit: Public residential KITTI seq02 check with vegetation and suburban scenery; not a substitute for dedicated orchard or non-urban multi-beam benchmarks.

fallback_on.note: Quadric-LO: quadric-surface (implicit q=xAx+bx+c) representation with point-to-quadric Taubin-distance residuals and plane fallback enabled; constant-velocity prior, no GT seed. mean_quadric/plane_corr=2863/19 plane_fallback_ratio=0.0066

fallback_off.note: Quadric-LO: quadric-surface (implicit q=xAx+bx+c) representation with point-to-quadric Taubin-distance residuals and plane fallback disabled; constant-velocity prior, no GT seed. mean_quadric/plane_corr=2862/0 plane_fallback_ratio=0.0000

conclusion: On public KITTI Odometry seq02 full (residential/suburban driving), Quadric-LO uses point-to-quadric constraints for 99.3% of correspondences (2863 quadric vs 19 plane corr/frame). Unlike the near-neutral seq00/07 highway ablation, disabling plane fallback on seq02 worsens RPE by +55.0% and ATE by +84.2% (0.933% / 30.98 m vs 1.446% / 57.06 m), showing that the rare plane fallback carries more weight on this vegetation-rich suburban sequence even though the quadric path dominates correspondence counts. Dedicated orchard or non-urban multi-beam benchmarks remain open before T0 promotion.
